meta.reportLabel: Wed - SOFT CPI FLIPS THE BOARD: June CPI -0.4% m/m (biggest drop since Apr 2020) -> 3.5% y/y, core FLAT at 2.6% - a broad disinflation surprise. July-hike odds collapsed to 12% (from 40%), DXY softened to ~100.9, and gold's haven bid FINALLY fired (+2.2% to ~$4,089). Oil kept climbing (WTI ~$81, Brent ~$87) as the Hormuz blockade took effect and Iran tests it. The oil shock still favors CAD & hurts EUR/JPY, but the soft-USD leg now stacks on top -> short USD/CAD is the cleanest trade, plus long USOIL, long gold, long CAD/JPY. Short EUR/USD downgraded to range. BoC/MPR today.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40 + XAU + XAG + USOIL
meta.regime: Yesterday's 'USD strong, gold failing' thesis was flipped by the data: June CPI came in far softer than feared. Headline fell -0.4% m/m (biggest since April 2020) to 3.5% y/y (from 4.2%, vs 3.8% exp), and core was FLAT m/m to 2.6% - a broad disinflation surprise. Fed July-hike odds collapsed to 12% (from 40%), the dollar softened (DXY ~100.9), and gold's haven bid finally fired (+2.2% to ~$4,089) as lower real-rate expectations met still-hot geopolitics. Oil kept climbing (WTI ~$81, Brent ~$87) as the Hormuz blockade took effect and Iran tests it. Net: the oil shock still favors CAD and hurts the energy-importers (EUR, JPY), but the soft-CPI dollar-down leg now stacks on top - making short USD/CAD the cleanest trade, alongside long USOIL, long gold, long CAD/JPY.
meta.nextBigEvent: Wed Jul 15 BoC decision + MPR (13:45 UTC, hold 2.25%; Macklem presser 14:45 UTC) - the MPR guidance is the mover. Then Sat Jul 18 Iran-Oman Hormuz talks are the off-ramp that can reverse oil/CAD/gold in minutes, and Mon Jul 21 NZ Q2 CPI (seen 4.1-4.5%, above RBNZ's 3.9% call) is the NZD swing.

dailyRead: Yesterday's 'USD strong, gold failing' thesis was flipped by the data: June CPI came in far softer than feared and reset the whole board. Headline fell -0.4% m/m - the biggest monthly drop since April 2020 - dragging the annual rate to 3.5% (from 4.2%, vs 3.8% expected), and, critically, core was FLAT m/m -> 2.6% y/y (from 2.9%) - a broad disinflation surprise, not just an energy-driven headline. Fed July-hike odds collapsed to 12% (from 40%) and Sept to 59% (from 74%); the dollar softened (DXY ~100.9, -0.4%; EUR/USD +0.4%) as the higher-for-longer trade partly unwound. The other regime change: gold's haven bid FINALLY fired - spot jumped +2.2% to ~$4,089 - because the soft print lowered real-rate expectations and the geopolitics stayed hot, so fear is now being priced through gold again, not just oil. And oil kept climbing: WTI ~$81 (+3.5%), Brent ~$87 (+4.5%) as CENTCOM's Hormuz blockade took effect and Iran began testing it (six sanctioned supertankers, ~12M barrels, slipping through with transponders off). Net: the oil supply-shock still favors CAD and hurts the energy-importers (EUR, JPY), but the soft-CPI dollar-down leg now stacks on top - making short USD/CAD the cleanest trade (both legs push it lower), alongside long USOIL, long gold, and CAD/JPY long. The one casualty: short EUR/USD is no longer clean - the dollar leg weakened and EUR bounced, so it downgrades to a two-way range. Cleanest leans: short USD/CAD, long USOIL, long XAU/USD - all still hostage to the Saturday Oman off-ramp and today's BoC/MPR.

today.headline: SOFT CPI flips the dollar - June -0.4% m/m -> 3.5%, core flat at 2.6%, hike odds crash to 12%; gold's haven fires (+2.2% to ~$4,089)
today.read: The disinflation surprise cut the higher-for-longer bid and softened DXY to ~100.9; it's a dovish lean, not a rout, into the Jul 28-29 FOMC (still a hold). The soft print also lit gold's long-dormant haven bid (XAU/USD -> LONG). The oil-inflation tail is the offset. Watch: hot July oil-CPI could re-fire the no-cut trade; a soft NFP revision extends the slide.

today.headline: Now the strongest - oil bid (WTI ~$81) AND a softer USD both push USD/CAD lower
today.read: The petro-bid stacks on the soft-CPI dollar-down leg, driving USD/CAD toward/under ~1.40 - the cleanest trade now. BoC decides today (hold, MPR). USD/CAD SHORT (HIGH), CAD/JPY LONG (MED). Risk: a Saturday Oman deal crushes oil; a dovish-surprise MPR.

today.headline: Short EUR/USD is no longer clean - soft USD relieved it off the 1-yr low (~1.145)
today.read: The dollar leg weakened and EUR bounced, but oil still drags the energy-importer laggard - the two legs offset, so EUR/USD downgrades from HIGH-conviction SHORT to a two-way range. Risk: a hawkish ECB Jul 23 hands it a leg; a re-firing USD/oil re-pins it toward 1.14.

today.headline: Swing catalyst got hotter - Q2 CPI (Jul 21) now seen 4.1-4.5%, ABOVE RBNZ's 3.9% call
today.read: Softer USD + risk-on relief help NZD, and a hot Q2 CPI would re-arm the hawks and challenge the dovish-hike. NZD/USD & NZD/JPY tilt up; NZD/CAD short holds on the oil leg. Risk: an oil-shock risk-off leg still hits high-beta NZD; a sub-4% print validates the dovish-hike.

today.headline: Pressure eases a touch - lower US real rates trim carry, USD/JPY only -0.2% to ~162
today.read: Still the biggest oil-shock loser, but the soft CPI cut US real rates and the returning haven bid is a tentative yen positive. RANGE on USD/JPY; CAD/JPY LONG holds on the oil leg. Risk: oil re-accelerates and re-grinds it toward 163/the MoF zone; a genuine panic snaps the haven bid on hard.

today.headline: No change - soft-USD relief vs oil-risk cap, thesis holds two-way
today.read: Sticky-core floor and a softer USD help at the margin, but the oil-shock risk-off leg caps it; LNG a light offset. Jun jobs Jul 16. MIXED. Risk: a re-escalation leg re-softens it; a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) re-arms the hawks.

today.headline: No change - firm rate story now with a soft-USD tailwind
today.read: Services CPI 3.7% keeps both hike dissents alive into Jul 30, and the softer dollar hands cable a tailwind, though risk-off keeps it a high-beta passenger. GBP/USD RANGE->long tilt. Risk: a risk-off flush; a firm-USD bounce; UK June CPI (~Jul 22).

strength.verdict: STRONGEST — oil bid + soft USD stack
strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (energy-driven; ex-energy 2.1%, trim ~2.0%); Jun jobs +18.2k beat (unemp 6.5% - all part-time, mfg -17k); BoC 2.25%, 5th straight hold; technical recession. The petro-currency bid (WTI ~$81) now compounds a softer USD after the soft CPI - both legs push USD/CAD lower.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ The oil bid + soft-USD tailwind drive USD/CAD toward/under ~1.40. BoC decides TODAY (hold 2.25%, MPR); internals hollow so guidance is the risk. Risk: a Saturday Oman deal crushes oil and the CAD bid; a dovish-surprise MPR; a risk-off flush hits CAD's risk leg. Next BoC (post-Jul) after today.

strength.tag: services CPI 3.7%; softer USD lifts cable
strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); headline 2.8% held; DMP wages 4.2%. BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (2 hike votes). The softer dollar hands cable a tailwind, though risk-off keeps GBP a high-beta passenger.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Services re-acceleration keeps the hike tail and both dissents alive into Jul 30; the soft USD helps near-term. Risk: a risk-off flush hits it; UK June CPI (~Jul 22) is the domestic check; a firm-USD bounce. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.verdict: SOFTER — CPI cuts the higher-for-longer bid
strength.drivers: June CPI -0.4% m/m (biggest drop since Apr 2020) -> 3.5% y/y (from 4.2%); core FLAT m/m -> 2.6% (from 2.9%) - a broad disinflation surprise. NFP +57k (Jun, big miss; unemp 4.2%). Fed 3.50-3.75% hold. July-hike odds collapsed to 12% (from 40%), Sept to 59% (from 74%); DXY softened to ~100.9.
strength.forward: 🔻⚖️ The disinflation surprise softened the dollar, but it's one print and the oil-inflation tail is live, so it's a dovish lean into the Jul 28-29 FOMC (still a hold). Risk: hot July oil-inflation data re-fires the no-cut trade; a fresh escalation haven bid firms DXY; a soft NFP revision extends the slide. Next FOMC Jul 28-29.

strength.verdict: FIRM — hot Q2 CPI risk re-arms hawks
strength.drivers: RBNZ hiked 25bp to 2.50% Jul 8, 6-0, but scripted dovish: headline seen PEAKED at 3.9% Q2, a 'considerably higher hurdle' for the next move. But economists now see Q2 CPI (Jul 21) at 4.1-4.5% - ABOVE that call - a hawkish risk. Soft USD + risk-on relief help at the margin.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Softer USD + risk-on help; the Jul 21 Q2 CPI is the swing - a hot print (4.1-4.5%) re-arms the hawks and challenges the dovish-hike. Risk: an oil-shock risk-off leg hits high-beta NZD; a sub-4% print validates the dovish-hike; an Oman deal. Next RBNZ Aug 19.

strength.verdict: MIXED — soft-USD relief vs oil hit
strength.drivers: EZ June flash HICP 2.8% (down from 3.2%) BUT core ROSE to 2.5% from 2.2%. Unemp 6.3%. ECB 2.25% depo (hiked Jun 11), meeting-by-meeting. The soft US CPI relieved EUR/USD off its 1-yr low, but the oil spike still drags the energy-importer laggard - the two legs offset.
strength.forward: 🔻⚖️ The clean short is gone: soft-USD relief vs an oil terms-of-trade drag = two-way. Oil-inflation lifts Sept-hike bets (a slow forward story). Risk: a hawkish Jul 23 ECB core tone hands EUR a leg; a re-firing dollar or oil re-pins it lower toward 1.14. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.tag: thin Aug tail (~36%); Jun jobs Jul 16
strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6%; headline 4.0%; unemp 4.5% loosening (June jobs Jul 16). RBA 4.35% (held Jun after 75bp of 2026 hikes); Aug 11 hike priced only ~36%. China CPI missed at +1%. Sticky-core floor + a softer USD help at the margin, but the oil-shock risk-off leg caps it; LNG a light offset.
strength.forward: 🔻⚖️ Soft-USD relief vs an oil risk-off cap = two-way. Risk: a re-escalation leg re-softens it; an Oman deal + a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) re-arm the hawks. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.verdict: WEAK — oil bill vs easing real rates
strength.tag: spot ~162; carry pressure eases a touch
strength.drivers: Core CPI 1.4% (May, subsidy-suppressed); unemp 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26 tight. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16, 7-1), guides more hikes. Still the biggest FX loser of the oil supply shock, but lower US real rates (soft CPI) trimmed the carry gap (USD/JPY only -0.2% to ~162) and the returning haven bid is a tentative positive. MoF verbal-only, Y11.73tn precedent unused.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Lower US real rates + a returning haven bid ease the pressure, but the oil bill keeps a floor; BoJ Jul 31 hawkish. Risk: oil re-accelerates and re-grinds it toward 163/the MoF zone; a genuine panic finally snaps the haven bid on hard. Next BoJ Jul 31.

symbols.risk: A Saturday Oman deal crushes oil and CAD; a JPY haven/panic snap or a MoF strike lifts the yen.

symbols.risk: A Saturday Oman deal unwinds it fast; stacked OPEC+/Saudi/Iran spare capacity + demand fear cap the top.

symbols.why: Soft USD + a hot-Q2-CPI-risk NZD tilt it up; risk-off caps.
symbols.risk: An oil-shock risk-off leg; a sub-4% NZ CPI Jul 21; a firm-USD bounce.

symbols.why: Both high-beta, but NZD carries the hotter near-term CPI catalyst - mild short tilt.

symbols.why: Oil-hit EUR vs a soft-USD-relieved AUD - two-way, no clean vector.

symbols.why: Firm GBP vs a risk-capped AUD - mild long tilt.

symbols.risk: An oil re-acceleration re-arms the energy-cost headwind; an Oman deal + soft USD re-fire the melt-up.

symbols.why: Silver (~$60) rallied with gold on the soft CPI + haven bid; the industrial-demand leg adds upside if growth fear fades.
symbols.risk: An oil-shock risk-off leg hits industrial demand; a real-rates re-lift drags the haven leg down with gold.

catalysts.note: Hold 2.25% (5th straight); Macklem presser 14:45 UTC. Oil bid + soft-USD tailwind vs hollow internals + technical recession - the MPR guidance is the mover.

catalysts.event: Trump's 20% Hormuz toll + US naval blockade
catalysts.note: Blockade of Iranian ports in effect; a demanded 20% toll on ALL Hormuz cargo - a structural supply-cost shock if enforced.

catalysts.event: Strait of Hormuz - Iran tests the blockade
catalysts.note: Six sanctioned supertankers (~12M bbl) slipping through with transponders off; a 'major test' brewing - every headline moves oil, CAD, JPY, gold, risk.
Unemployment 4.5% loosening; feeds the Aug 11 RBA calculus.
Iranian officials travel to Oman on the lane/territorial-waters dispute - the live off-ramp; a deal crushes the oil/CAD/gold premium, a breakdown re-spikes it.
Economists see 4.1-4.5% - ABOVE RBNZ's 'peaked at 3.9%' call; a hot print re-arms the hawks and challenges the dovish-hike.
Hold; core-2.5% + the oil-inflation spike is the Sept-hike signal.
Warsh round 2; hold + soft CPI cut hike odds to 12%, but oil-inflation keeps the no-cut tail.
Dissent count for GBP after services 3.7%.
Guidance with spot ~162 and the MoF zone near; the oil bill still the drag.

geopolitics: The war-shock still drives oil, but the macro story flipped underneath it. CENTCOM's naval blockade of Iranian ports took effect (4 PM ET Tuesday), and rather than backing down, Iran began testing it - six US-sanctioned supertankers capable of carrying ~12 million barrels have slipped through the strait into the Gulf of Oman in the past week with transponders off, after Washington revoked Tehran's temporary permission to sell oil on July 7. CNN reports Iran 'appears to be preparing a major test' of the blockade. Trump's demanded 20% toll on ALL Hormuz cargo remains a structural supply-cost overhang. The one off-ramp is unchanged: Iranian officials travel to Oman on Saturday for talks on the lane/territorial-waters dispute, with indirect US-Iran contact via Qatar - so a reversal is still one headline away, but for now the strait stays contested and the premium stays bid. FX transmission (Wednesday - soft CPI overlays the oil shock): oil extended its rally - WTI ~$81 (+3.5%), Brent ~$87 (+4.5%) - building on Monday's six-year record gain. CAD is still the FX winner, and now doubly so: the petro-bid stacks on a softer USD to press USD/CAD toward/under ~1.40. The big change is the USD, which softened (DXY ~100.9, -0.4%; EUR/USD +0.4%) after June CPI printed far cooler than feared - headline -0.4% m/m (biggest drop since April 2020) to 3.5% y/y, and core flat at 2.6% - collapsing July-hike odds to 12% (from 40%). And gold's haven bid finally fired: spot +2.2% to ~$4,089, because the soft print lowered real-rate expectations and the geopolitics stayed hot - so fear is being priced through gold again, reversing the 'haven keeps failing' read; silver rallied alongside (~$60). EUR and JPY, the energy-importers, get partial relief from the weaker dollar - EUR bounced off its 1-yr low, and the yen's carry pressure eased (USD/JPY only -0.2% to ~162) - but the oil bill still caps both. AUD/NZD catch a soft-USD risk-on bid, capped by the oil-shock risk-off leg; GER40 steadies as risk-on relief offsets the oil-cost headwind. The board is now oil-shock + soft-CPI, which rotates conviction to the trades where both forces align: short USD/CAD, long USOIL, long XAU/USD, long CAD/JPY - while short EUR/USD downgrades to range as its dollar leg weakened. Everything oil-linked stays hostage to the Saturday Oman headline.
